items.title: Wall Street Is Abandoning Bets on a Stronger Euro
items.summary: Wall Street banks are capitulating on bets for a stronger euro, as markets see the US outpacing Europe on interest-rate hikes for the rest of this year.

items.title: The World Is Racing to Develop New Nuclear Fuels
US Futures Climb on Reports Peace Talks to Resume: Markets Wrap
items.summary: US equity futures climbed after reports that the US and Iran stepped back from an escalation in hostilities that had threatened the fragile ceasefire underpinning peace talks.

items.title: With a Fragile Ceasefire under Threat, What Future for the Strait of Hormuz?
items.summary: The U.S.-Israeli war with Iran prompted Tehran to close the Strait of Hormuz, allowing only selected ships to pass through. In this Q&A, Crisis Group experts Yasmine Farouk, Chris Newton and Ali Vaez consider what could come next in this vital maritime trade corridor.

items.title: Iran warns ships not to bypass its chosen Hormuz route
items.summary:
Iran’s top diplomat warned on Sunday that any attempt by ships to bypass its preferred route through the Strait of Hormuz would “increase tensions” in the Middle East, as US and Iranian forces again traded attacks.
The exchanges underscored the fragility of a Pakistan-brokered agreement aimed at ending the war launched by the United States and Israel in February that disrupted shipping through the

items.title: The Path To A September Rate Cut ( Despite AI Inflation )
Oil Jumps After Tanker Hit in Middle East Flare-Up
items.summary: Oil advanced after a tanker carrying Qatari crude was hit during a flare-up in attacks between the US and Iran, straining a ceasefire and hampering shipping through the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Serbians keep up protests after President Vucic says he will step down
items.summary:
Thousands of protesters descended on the Serbian city of Kraljevo on Sunday, keeping up pressure on President Aleksandar Vucic a day after he said he would step down ‌within weeks to pave the way for early presidential and parliamentary elections.
Although many protesters expressed satisfaction with Vucic’s resignation, they fear he may not fully relinquish power. Analysts say he may try to run fo

EU and NATO Members Grapple with War in Iran
items.summary: This week on War & Peace, Olga speaks with Crisis Group’s head of EU Affairs, Lisa Musiol, about how the EU, its members and European members of NATO have responded to the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran and what it reveals about EU foreign policy and its constraints.

items.title: Iran Risks Peace Talks With U.S. to Maintain Leverage Over Strait
items.summary: Iran sees its control over the Strait of Hormuz as critical leverage in peace talks with the United States. It seems willing to risk the cease-fire to maintain that power.

items.title: Mideast Live Updates: Dispute Over Strait Deepens as U.S. and Iran Trade Attacks
items.summary: In a fourth day of hostilities, Iran said that it had targeted a U.S. naval base in Bahrain and a Kuwaiti air base with drones and missiles. No major damage or casualties were reported.
